Lantern: truth reflected in a flawless mirror
a bright intelligence that banishes confusion
language that unveils what was concealed
the eye that does not blink
understanding as a beam through obscurity
cobalt light in the house without walls
a question asked by the glory
reason sharpened into a blade of light
the opening of the eye of the mind
the brilliance that cannot be ignored
Winter: silence and stillness
cold endings
forgetting and quiet
motionless suspension
the beauty of the final hush
death without drama
memory preserved in frost
the peace that follows extinction
the cold that ends all fever
voices stilled beneath snow
the dead remembered in silence
winter as an ending made graceful
stillness deeper than sleep
elegy, frost, and remembrance
the white calm of the grave
the hush that follows the last breath
memory surviving after life is gone
a severe and perfect quiet
the season of endings
frozen beauty and the discipline of loss
silence gathered around the dead
the chill that preserves what remains
the final stillness of a closed room
all warmth departed, all noise spent
the cold mirror of mortality
a remnant of grief held motionless
the solemn dignity of an ending
snow over footprints, silence over names
the dead who do not speak but remain
an ending so complete it becomes beautiful
Knock: opening thresholds
passage through barriers
crossing a sealed boundary
entry by force or right
the wound that becomes a door
every lock implies a key
the gate opened by blood and knowledge
passage into forbidden chambers
the art of unsealing
the secret doctrine of opening
keys, scars, and thresholds
the hinge yielding under a true name
the invitation hidden in every barrier
doors that answer to wounds
the crossing from without to within
the boundary made permeable
intrusion into the hidden house
serpents, wounds, and the opened way
locks undone by understanding
the moment before the sealed thing gives way
the body marked into a threshold
a scar that teaches entry
the key that is also an oracle
the means by which ascent is permitted
the passage between one state and another
knuckles on wood, blood on the latch
the law that every closure may be broken
an opening cut where none was offered
the threshold crossed at cost
what opens the way for all other powers
Forge: fire that shapes and remakes
destruction in the service of transformation
the heat that remakes form
dawn born from ruin
the furnace where the old self is consumed
hammer, flame, and transfiguration
the bright violence of necessary change
what is broken down to be made anew
the smith's law of remaking
fire as creation through ruin
the blaze that ends the night
metals softened for a stronger shape
the scar of change accepted willingly
the forge where identity is recast
purification by intolerable heat
the act of making through burning away
destructive change that leaves a truer form
flame, craft, and merciless improvement
the foundry of impossible futures
the anvil beneath a merciless sun
remaking the house by fire
the artisan's cruelty toward imperfection
to unmake in order to make
the red work of transformation
the dawn that follows combustion
a shape driven out of raw matter
fire as command rather than comfort
the smithing of worlds and bodies alike
the hand that divides and reshapes
what survives the furnace emerges changed
Grail: the craving that grows by feeding
the mouth that is never finally satisfied
the thirst that makes reason kneel
to consume and to be consumed
the body as chalice and wound
the pulse of life bent toward indulgence
the lush cruelty of wanting more
the cup filled with blood and promise
the scent of fruit just before corruption
to taste, to bleed, to yearn again
the red law that every longing seeks a mouth
Moth: chaos and yearning
passion without obedience
concealment in the dark wood
transformation through shedding
the buzzing in the brain
instinct before reason
the wild answer to the light
loss that becomes metamorphosis
a path found by wandering
madness with a hidden purpose
the scissors that free the truer shape
desire stripped to something primal
the skin discarded for a stranger form
secrets kept in velvet darkness
confusion that conceals a deeper truth
erratic motion toward forbidden radiance
the self cut away in necessary pieces
yearning that cannot explain itself
a dance of obscurity and appetite
the impulse to flee the known path
deception, instinct, and holy misdirection
what is unnecessary must be shed
feathers, fur, skin, and names left behind
the perilous wisdom of losing oneself
mad desire circling a distant light
concealment as a mode of power
the old shape sloughing off in moonlit confusion
sacrifice by cutting, shaving, or stripping away
the wood's wild doctrine of change
a truth that arrives wearing madness
